ECU.CRITICAL_NOTE: ECU scored 0 goals in 2 WC2026 matches despite 16 total SOT from 39 total shots

market_calibrated.note: Calibrated to match Smarkets FTR: GER=61.6%, Draw=19.6%, ECU=18.4%

context_flags: GER_ROTATION: Germany qualified (6pts), will likely rotate starters → lower GER attacking output, possibly lower defensive line
ECU_MUST_WIN: Ecuador has scored 0 goals in 2 WC2026 matches, this is their last chance
ECU_FINISHING_CRISIS: 16 total SOT across 2 matches = 0 goals (catastrophic conversion rate)
GER_DEEP_BLOCK: Rotating Germany likely plays lower defensive line → fewer ECU offsides
ECU_OFFSIDE_HISTORY: 0 offsides in GD1, 1 in GD2 (λ=0.5 avg) → very unlikely ≥2 offsides
MARKET_DISCOUNT: Market prices GER at 61.6% despite only 90 Elo pts advantage - reflects ECU's poor form
rules_applied: RULE_OFFSIDE_DEFENSIVE_SHAPE: GER rotating = likely deep/low line → cap ECU offsides at 0.10
WINNING_TRAIL: Use ESPN verified data, submit near-raw Poisson/Skellam, no stacked narrative adjustments

questions.text: At halftime, will both teams have at least 1 shot on target?
questions.context: GER almost certainly SOT in 1H. ECU attacks from start (must-win). ECU avg 8 SOT → ~4/half. Even in GD1 (1 total SOT) they likely had some 1H attempt. Confidence: medium-high.

questions.text: Will Ecuador commit more fouls than Germany?
questions.model_skellam: P(ECU > GER in fouls) = 33.3% (Skellam with λ_ECU=10, λ_GER=11.5)
questions.context: GER rotation may reduce fouls. ECU must-win = tactical pressing = more fouls. Net effect modest. GER averages higher fouls than ECU in WC2026.

questions.text: In the second half, will Ecuador have more corner kicks than Germany?
questions.data_note: No ESPN corner data available for WC2026 matches
questions.context: Key structural driver: GER leads in 61.6% of scenarios → sits back in 2H → ECU chases → more ECU corners. But GER quality still generates corners too. Estimate without verified data.

questions.text: Will Germany score more goals than Ecuador in the second half?
questions.context: Counter-intuitively low at 46.1% because 2H 0-0 ties are common (39.3%). GER is stronger but with small λ values, ties dominate. Crowd will over-price GER dominance in 2H at 55-65%.
questions.confidence: medium - crowd likely over-bullish on GER 2H dominance

questions.text: Will Germany have more shots on target than Ecuador in the second half?
questions.context: GER rotation lowers their SOT rate. ECU must-win brings maximum attacking intensity → more ECU 2H SOT. ECU's GD2 survival context (15 SOT) is the relevant comparison. Adjustment downward from raw.
questions.confidence: low - rotation + must-win context creates major uncertainty

questions.text: Will a penalty kick be awarded OR a red card be shown?
questions.context: WC2026 so far: 0 RC in our 4 observed matches. High-stakes survival match for ECU increases foul rate and drama. Historical WC group stage base ~25-30% per match for penalty or RC.
questions.confidence: low - rare event, hard to model

questions.text: Will Ecuador be caught offside 2 or more times?
questions.context: CRITICAL FLAG: ECU has 0 offsides in GD1 and 1 in GD2 (λ=0.5). GER rotation = likely lower defensive line = NO high-line offside trap. Deep block eliminates offside traps. Even in ECU's most attacking game (15 SOT vs CUR), they only had 1 offside. STRONG NO signal.

questions.text: Will both teams score AND the match have 3 or more total goals?
questions.context: ECU scored 0 in 2 WC2026 matches. BTTS YES = 45.7% (market). P(BTTS ∩ ≥3 goals) bounded by OVER 2.5 (33.9%). Many GER wins are clean sheets (2-0, 3-0). P(BTTS|3+ goals) ≈ 55-60%.

questions.text: Will Ecuador score at least 1 goal?
questions.market_anchor: BTTS YES = 45.7% → P(ECU scores) ≥ 45.7% (since BTTS requires ECU to score)
questions.context: ECU MUST WIN and will attack maximally. BUT: scored 0 goals in 2 WC2026 matches. P(ECU ≥1) = P(BTTS) + P(ECU wins with GER clean sheet) ≈ 46% + 2% ≈ 48-52%. Market-anchored estimate.
questions.confidence: low - ECU's 0-goal form is significant but must-win context changes urgency

questions.text: Will the match have 2 or fewer total goals?
questions.context: Market UNDER 2.5 = 66.5%. ECU's 0-goal form supports low scoring game. GER rotation also reduces their output. Both factors push toward UNDER. Strong structural case for UNDER 2.5.

strongest_signals: Q7: ECU ≥2 offsides = 0.10 (STRONG NO - low λ=0.5 + GER deep block context)
Q8: BTTS+3+ = 0.22 (NO - bounded by market OVER 2.5 at 33.9%)

Q2_ECU_more_fouls.note: Second-biggest loss of the match. We trusted the WC2026 foul averages (ECU 10.0 < GER 11.5) and bet against a rotated-Germany attacking more fouls, but Ecuador's must-win pressing intensity drove much heavier fouling than their history suggested. The crowd's 57% correctly weighted the must-win urgency far more than we did.

Q4_GER_more_2H_goals.note: Our 2H-tie-heavy Poisson model (46%, below the crowd's bullish 54% on a rotated Germany) was directionally right.

Q7_ECU_offside_2plus.note: Biggest single win of the match. RULE_OFFSIDE_DEFENSIVE_SHAPE (rotated Germany plays a lower defensive line, eliminating the offside trap) held exactly as modeled against a crowd that priced it near coin-flip.

Q8_BTTS_3plus_goals.note: Biggest loss of the match. We anchored hard to Ecuador's '0 goals in 2 WC2026 matches' finishing crisis and a market-bounded ceiling (OU2.5=33.9%), but Ecuador finally converted in their must-win finale, scoring twice. The 0-goal streak was a small-sample artifact (16 SOT, 0 goals = unsustainable conversion luck), not a durable skill signal — we over-weighted it.

Q10_under_2_5_goals.note: Third-biggest loss. Same root cause as Q8 — we doubled down on Ecuador's poor finishing continuing into a third match, when in fact a must-win elimination scenario was exactly the moment a finishing crisis was most likely to break.

key_confirmations: Q7 (+21.43) shows RULE_OFFSIDE_DEFENSIVE_SHAPE works well for a rotated/defensively passive favorite, independent of the underdog's own attacking intent.

Q2, Q8, Q10 (combined -67.13) share one root cause: treating Ecuador's 0-goals-in-2-matches finishing crisis as a stable rate rather than a small-sample (16 SOT, 0 goals) variance artifact. A must-win elimination match is precisely the highest-motivation, highest-regression-to-the-mean scenario — we should have shaded UP Ecuador's scoring odds and DOWN our confidence in the under, not doubled down on the streak.
